text:
In this work, we synthesized two novel pyrazole derivatives—2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (Pyz-1) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (Pyz-2)—and characterized their structures using 1H-NMR, 13C-NMR, FT-IR, and mass spectrometry. To better understand their chemical behavior, we performed density functional theory (DFT) calculations using the B3LYP/6-311++G** level of theory, predicting the molecular geometry, electronic properties, and thermodynamic stability of both compounds in the gas phase and aqueous solution. 

Bioactivity assessments revealed that Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 possess significant antidiabetic and antioxidant potential. Both compounds effectively inhibited α-glucosidase and α-amylase, demonstrating IC50 values (75.62–95.85 µM and 119.3–120.2 µM, respectively) comparable to the standard drug Acarbose. Furthermore, the derivatives showed powerful xanthine oxidase inhibition, with Pyz-2 (IC50 = 10.75 µM) notably outperforming the clinical standard Allopurinol. Antioxidant testing via DPPH, ABTS, FRAP, and H2O2 assays further confirmed their strong radical scavenging capabilities. To elucidate the mechanism of action, we conducted molecular docking simulations, which revealed favorable binding energies and specific amino acid interactions within the active sites of α-glucosidase and α-amylase. Overall, these findings suggest that Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 are promising multi-target candidates for managing diabetes and its associated oxidative complications.
